name: Flipkart Product Page — Timeout & Fallback Resilience
scenario: Flipkart product page calls 3 services: recommendation engine (1.5s timeout), review service (1s), related products (1s). Add @TimeLimiter to each with appropriate fallbacks so the page always loads within 2 seconds.

core.description: application.yml: 3 timelimiter configs + 3 circuitbreaker configs. cancelRunningFuture=true on all.
ProductPageService: 3 methods, each with @TimeLimiter + @CircuitBreaker returning CompletableFuture. All fallbacks return safe defaults.
loadPage(productId): calls all 3 in parallel using CompletableFuture.allOf, aggregates results.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): page loads when all up, page loads when any one fails, parallel calls complete faster than sequential.
README: explains parallel vs sequential and why cancelling the running future matters.

bonus.description: HTTP client timeouts: configure RestTemplate with connection=2s, read=5s for each downstream call. Test with WireMock simulating slow server.
TimeoutMonitor: tracks TimeoutException count per CB. Alert if > 10 timeouts/minute.
Add @Bulkhead(max=50) outside @TimeLimiter on the recommendation call. Test: bulkhead full before timeout fires.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. @TimeLimiter REQUIRES CompletableFuture return type. cancelRunningFuture=true: without this, the thread keeps running even after timeout is reported (wastes resources). Parallel pattern: start all CompletableFutures BEFORE calling .get() on any of them. Fallback return type must also be CompletableFuture<T>. Stack order: @TimeLimiter (outer) > @CircuitBreaker (inner).
